verdicts.issue: Key year (2016) is the arXiv preprint year (arXiv:1611.03530); body year (2017) is the ICLR conference year. Within preprint-vs-conference tolerance per bib-check.md §5.

verdicts.note: Stamp present, not re-audited per audit instructions. (Spot-check observation: stamped entry contains questionable metadata — publisher=Springer US, pages=312--312, date=2011, doi=10.1007/978-0-387-30164-8_251 — which conflict with the actual ICLR 2018 mixup paper (arXiv:1710.09412). Worth flagging for re-stamp pass.)

verdicts.issue: Key year (2019) is the arXiv preprint year (arXiv:1911.09251); body date (2020-04-03) is the AAAI 2020 publication date. Within preprint-vs-conference tolerance.

Severe key/content mismatch (§5 silent-corruption class). Key prefix says 'zhang2019root' but body is a 2023 paper by Wang Di et al. titled 'A Lightweight convolutional neural network for nicotine prediction in tobacco by near-infrared spectroscopy' (Frontiers in Plant Science, vol 14, doi:10.3389/fpls.2023.1138693). First-author surname (Wang) ≠ key surname (zhang); year (2023) ≠ key year (2019), gap > 1. Any [@zhang2019root] citation in prose will render as '(Wang et al., 2023)'. Likely the original 'zhang2019root' content was overwritten by copy-paste from a different entry without updating the key.

suggested_fix.note: Rename key to match content, then audit all .qmd files for [@zhang2019root] citations and update them to [@wang2023nicotine] (or to the originally intended Zhang 2019 paper if one exists). Use: rg '\[@zhang2019root\]|@zhang2019root\b' book/quarto/ to find call sites before propagating the rename.

verdicts.issue: Missing pages field. CVPRW 2020 paper has pages 692-693 (or DOI-based locator). Booktitle should be expanded per §2: 'IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)' -> already close, but inconsistent expansion across the chunk.

suggested_fix.note: Add pages = {692--693} (per IEEE Xplore listing); confirm at https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9150646/.

verdicts.issue: Could not locate this paper via web search. Body has a generic title ('Optimizing Memory Access for Deep Learning Workloads'), initials-only authors ('Zhang, Y. and Li, J. and Ouyang, H.'), and impossibly mixed venue fields: journal = IEEE TCAD AND booktitle = MICRO 2020 simultaneously (a paper is in one venue, not both). No DOI, no URL. The closest real paper is 'Optimizing Memory-Access Patterns for Deep Learning Accelerators' by Hongbin Zheng et al. (arXiv:2002.12798, 2020) — different title, different authors. This entry has the smell of an LLM hallucination from an earlier auto-bib pass.

suggested_fix.note: Investigate: (a) confirm whether this entry was hand-cited or auto-generated, (b) if it's a stand-in for Zheng et al. 2020, replace the entry and rename key to zheng2020memoryaccess, (c) if no paper backs it, remove the entry and the citing prose. Use: rg '\[@zhang2020optimizing\]|@zhang2020optimizing\b' book/quarto/ to find call sites.

verdicts.issue: Could not locate this paper via web search. Title 'Learning-based Efficient Sparsity and Quantization for Neural Network Compression' attributed to Zhang Yi et al. in IEEE TNNLS vol 32 no 9 pp 3980-3994 (2021) does not match any TNNLS article I could find. No DOI, no URL. Author list and venue specifics look plausible, but the paper does not surface in searches by title or by author+venue. Possibly a fabricated entry, possibly a real paper with a slightly drifted title.

suggested_fix.note: Investigate: search IEEE Xplore directly for Yi Zhang TNNLS 2021 sparsity quantization papers; if no match, remove. If a real paper is found, add DOI and URL. Use: rg '\[@zhang2021learning\]|@zhang2021learning\b' book/quarto/ to find call sites before any rename/removal.

verdicts.issue: Currently stamped as arXiv preprint only. Paper was accepted as ICML 2024 Oral; could be upgraded to @inproceedings with PMLR publisher, but arXiv form is acceptable per §4 (preprints exempt from page-number requirement).

Author list is partially fabricated. Entry claims authors 'Zheng, Lianmin and Jia, Ziheng and Gao, Yida and Lin, Jiacheng and Han, Song and Geng, Xuehai and Zhao, Eric and Wu, Tianqi'. The actual Ansor (OSDI 2020) authors per USENIX and DBLP are: Lianmin Zheng, Chengfan Jia, Minmin Sun, Zhao Wu, Cody Hao Yu, Ameer Haj-Ali, Yida Wang, Jun Yang, Danyang Zhuo, Koushik Sen, Joseph E. Gonzalez, Ion Stoica. Only Lianmin Zheng's name matches; the rest are wrong (Jia first name wrong, Sun missing, Han Song NOT a coauthor, Gonzalez/Stoica missing, etc.). Also the key 'Zheng2020' uses CamelCase in violation of the project's surname[year][topicword] lowercase convention; should be zheng2020ansor.

suggested_fix.note: Replace author field with the verified DBLP author list, and rename key. Find call sites: rg '\[@Zheng2020\]|@Zheng2020\b' book/quarto/ — expect direct uppercase matches; update each [@Zheng2020] to [@zheng2020ansor].

verdicts.issue: Entry stamps as arXiv preprint; paper was also accepted to ICLR 2021 (poster). Could optionally be upgraded to @inproceedings with publisher = OpenReview.net per §3, but arXiv form is acceptable. Key is unusually long but readable.

Paper content (Trained Ternary Quantization, Zhu/Han/Mao/Dally, ICLR 2017) is correct, but the entry is thin: missing booktitle, publisher, url. Per §3, ICLR entries need publisher = {OpenReview.net} and a booktitle. Also key uses CamelCase 'Zhu2017' instead of the project convention zhu2017trained or zhu2017ternary.

suggested_fix.note: Change @article -> @inproceedings; add booktitle = {International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)}, publisher = {OpenReview.net}, url = {https://openreview.net/forum?id=S1_pAu9xl}. Rename key from CamelCase Zhu2017 to zhu2017ternary; find call sites with: rg '\[@Zhu2017\]|@Zhu2017\b' book/quarto/.

verdicts.issue: Missing url field; should add https://openreview.net/forum?id=r1Ue8Hcxg or arXiv:1611.01578 per §4 (URL required when no DOI).
